denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/14/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W103492279          Group #: GRP-1441
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-164438
Claim #: CLM-2026-211374
Date of Service (requested): 03/25/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Plan Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 03/27/2026 for Broström ligament repair related to Ankle instability, chronic lateral (M25.371). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Broström ligament repair
Diagnosis (as submitted): Ankle instability, chronic lateral (M25.371)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0101 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This determination is based on a plan exclusion. State coverage mandates do not alter the terms of this plan for this determination.
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0101 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Clinical criteria for Broström ligament repair are not met per the cited utilization guideline module for Ankle instability, chronic lateral (M25.371). The request does not satisfy threshold requirements in the referenced criteria set.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with guideline mis cite. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director before filing a written appeal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
Appeals contact (as listed): Appeals Unit, P.O. Box 14582, Lexington, KY 40512-4582; Fax: (877) 555-0199.
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W103492279) and reference UM-2026-164438. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Ankle instability, chronic lateral (M25.371). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Broström ligament repair recommended as the next appropriate step. Repeated inversion injuries; MRI ATFL attenuation. The prior-authorization packet included dated clinic notes, medication trials, and objective findings supporting medical necessity. The denial rationale does not reference those submissions. Treating clinicians document ongoing functional impairment and recommend proceeding without further delay when benefit-plan criteria have been met based on the record on file.
